title: 'Wanted papers': Drunk son kills father over Rs 150cr property dispute in Ghaziabad
content: A 32-year-old man allegedly shot his father dead after a heated argument. The dispute began when the father confronted his son for returning home drunk. A land dispute was also ongoing between the father and his elder son. The son then fired multiple bullets at his father, killing him instantly. Police have registered a murder case and are searching for the accused.

title: Another setback for TMC, Koel Mallick resigns from Rajya Sabha
content: TMC leader Koel Mallik resigned from the Rajya Sabha on Thursday. This follows Madan Mitra's departure and joining a rebel TMC faction. Earlier, Sukhendu Sekhar Ray and Sushmita Dev also resigned from the party. They subsequently joined the Bharatiya Janata Party and filed nomination papers. The BJP defended its decision to field these former TMC leaders.

title: China charms world, now more popular than US; Xi tops Trump on trust: Report
content: Recent global surveys reveal that China's image is increasingly favorable compared to that of the United States. Under Xi Jinping's leadership, he commands greater trust internationally than Donald Trump does. While personal freedoms remain a strength for the US, this advantage is slowly diminishing as China is perceived to engage less in interventionist policies worldwide. These trends underscore a noteworthy shift in international perceptions.

title: Falkland poster row spills over: British govt seeks FIFA action against Argentina
content: Argentina defeated England 2-1 in the FIFA World Cup semifinal on Wednesday in Atlanta. During the post-match celebrations, Argentine players posed with a banner handed to them by supporters in the stands that read, "Las Malvinas son Argentinas" ("The Malvinas are Argentine"). Argentina refers to the Falkland Islands as Islas Malvinas.

title: Google founder’s net worth crossed $300bn as Warren Buffet admits mistake
content: Larry Page's net worth exceeded three hundred billion dollars after Alphabet's stock climbed.  This significant rise added billions to the co-founder's considerable personal wealth.  Warren Buffett recently admitted he regretted not investing in Alphabet sooner.  Berkshire Hathaway initiated its first investment in Alphabet last year.  That initial investment has since grown substantially in value.

title: 'Diego is enjoying this immensely from up there': Messi's emotional tribute to Maradona
content: Argentina secured a place in the FIFA World Cup 2026 final after defeating England. Lionel Messi provided two assists, enabling Argentina to overcome a deficit. He dedicated the significant victory to the legendary Diego Maradona. This win mirrors Maradona's iconic performance against England forty years prior. Argentina will now face Spain in the championship match.

title: Messi magic and tactical panic: How England engineered their semi-final downfall
content: Thomas Tuchel’s plan worked until England scored, parked the bus and invited Lionel Messi to spend 37 minutes dismantling it. Read how England engineered its own downfall in today's Offside.

title: Jeff Bezos' ex-wife MacKenzie Scott donates to US youth mental health non-profit
content: MacKenzie Scott has donated twenty million dollars to Active Minds. This significant contribution is the largest in the organization's history. Active Minds works with young people to improve mental health awareness and reduce stigma. The unrestricted funds will expand programs and support youth leaders. Scott continues her extensive charitable giving after selling Amazon shares.

title: Army chief visits crucial Siliguri Corridor - why Chicken's Neck area is critical for India
content: India is actively enhancing infrastructure to secure its vital land bridge connecting the mainland to Northeast states. Railways plans a new underground line, and highway expansion is accelerating through central agencies. Land has been transferred to border security forces for improved surveillance and troop movement. The Army Chief reviewed security dynamics and modernization initiatives in the Eastern Command. He also outlined his vision for an agile and future-ready Indian Army.

title: $22.5M shack near Robert De Niro’s estate comes with rare oceanfront lot
content: An oceanfront Montauk property is listed for twenty-two point five million dollars. This desirable location sits adjacent to Robert De Niro's estate and offers unique advantages. The sale includes approved plans for a large modern home, bypassing lengthy permit processes. This property provides an opportunity to build a significant residence on one point one acres. The site offers incredible ocean views, similar to those found in Malibu.

title: How a Buddha tattoo nearly got a man killed in Myanmar prison
content: Arnold Putra encountered a harrowing situation in Myanmar, facing arrest for having a Buddha tattoo. Initially apprehended due to his connections with student groups labeled as terrorists by the military junta, he endured intense pressure to confess to assisting rebels. Transferred to a labor camp, his predicament worsened until an official from the Indonesian embassy intervened, facilitating a modification of his tattoo to deter further issues.

title: Judge blocks Trump's visa curbs for foreign researchers, trust & safety experts
content: A US federal judge has blocked an immigration policy targeting foreign researchers. This policy could deny visas or deport professionals working on misinformation. The judge ruled the policy likely violates the First Amendment's free speech protections. The decision affects individuals linked to US-based organizations combating digital hate. This ruling prevents the government from enforcing the policy during ongoing legal proceedings.

title: Renting a home without a written agreement? Here's why it may be risky
content: In India, verbal rental agreements often lead to misunderstandings, as they lack essential written terms and legal safeguards. This scenario exposes tenants to unpredictable rent increases and abrupt evictions. The absence of documentation complicates security deposits, and property repairs frequently result in disputes. To protect both parties, written contracts are now a requirement under the Model Tenancy Act in states that have adopted it.

title: 100% US tariff threat over Russian oil: India may have reasons to stay calm
content: This is not the first attempt by the US to penalise countries purchasing Russian oil. Last year, Washington imposed an additional 25% tariff on India over its imports of Russian crude, before reversing the measure in February.

title: Jon Gruden on Antonio Brown: 'He finished everything' and NFL's future
content: Jon Gruden discussed his biggest coaching regret, the Antonio Brown situation with the Raiders. He expressed concern over modern NFL offenses, which he feels lack communication. ​During a recent appearance on Cam Heyward's Not Just Football podcast, the Super Bowl winning coach reflected on one of the biggest disappointments of his career and shared why he believes today's NFL is moving in the wrong direction.

title: Roman Reigns vs Seth Rollins net worth 2026: Salary, brand deals compared
content: Roman Reigns and Seth Rollins will clash at SummerSlam 2026 in a highly anticipated match. Reigns boasts an estimated net worth of twenty million dollars and earns fifteen million annually. Rollins holds an estimated net worth between nine and twelve million dollars, earning seven million. Both wrestlers have lucrative WWE contracts and significant merchandise sales contributing to their wealth.

title: Erling Haaland's 'five girlfriends' comment: The viral twist explained
content: Erling Haaland's old "five girlfriends" comment has resurfaced, surprising fans years after it first made headlines. While many initially thought the Norway striker was talking about his dating life, he was actually referring to the five footballs he kept after scoring hat-tricks. The lighthearted remark highlighted his obsession with scoring goals long before he became a global superstar, Manchester City's leading striker and one of football's highest-paid players.

title: Smith-Pooran showdown: Record breaking MLC game creates T20 history with ...
content: Nicholas Pooran, Andries Gous and Steven Smith all reached three figures in a game that rewrote multiple T20 records. While Pooran smashed a 31-ball hundred — the fastest century in Major League Cricket history — Gous and Smith responded with 40-ball hundreds each.

title: 'We will kill Trump': Tehran puts up gigantic billboard depicting US president in coffin
content: A large billboard showing Donald Trump in a coffin appeared in Tehran's Enghelab Square. This display coincided with renewed military strikes between the United States and Iran. US forces conducted strikes targeting Iranian command centers and air defense systems. Iranian authorities reported a missile strike near a cancer hospital in Ahvaz. Damage was also reported at an industrial facility in Qeshm following an enemy attack.

title: Messi, space & Argentina's relentless pressure: How final 10 mins broke England
content: Messi produced two assists in the last minutes as Argentina came from behind to beat England 2-1 on Wednesday (local time) and book a place in Sunday's World Cup final against Spain. Enzo Fernandez equalised in the 85th minute before Lautaro Martinez headed home Messi's cross in the second minute of stoppage time to complete another Argentina comeback.

title: Passport lost, stranded alone in Europe: Survival of Md Imran, India's upcoming GM
content: At 16, Indian chess prodigy Md Imran was stranded in Slovakia after a bus carrying his passport, visas and belongings left without him. Despite the ordeal, financial struggles and lack of institutional support, he played on to earn two Grandmaster norms. Now on the verge of becoming India's newest GM, Imran urgently awaits reissuance of his US visa to join his scholarship at UTRGV before August 23.

title: IRCTC's new website goes live: How it makes ticket booking easier for consumers
content: The Indian railway catering and tourism corporation has launched a beta version of its new website. This revamped portal offers a cleaner interface and a faster checkout process for passengers. Saved passenger details will enable quicker repeat bookings and a more convenient user experience. The ministry seeks user feedback to refine the platform before its final rollout. This improved system will soon integrate with the new Passenger Reservation Engine.

title: E20 fuel damages Maruti car; company ordered to give customer a new vehicle
content: A consumer court ordered Maruti Suzuki to replace a Grand Vitara with an E20-compatible model. The complainant alleged technical issues after E20 petrol became available. The court found the manufacturer and dealer guilty of deficiency in service. Maruti Suzuki must replace the SUV or pay its full value. The company also faces a compensation payment for mental agony.

title: 111 missiles, 0 hits: From Barak-8 to SM‑6 — Inside the layered defence of modern warships
content: Naval vessels face numerous threats, necessitating advanced defensive systems for protection. Modern warships employ missiles and guns to counter aerial and anti-ship threats. The United States uses maritime pressure as a lever against Iran today. Iranian missile salvos were likely intercepted by Standard-series missiles. These systems defend warships and merchant vessels from various attacks.

title: 'Stressful for students': SC tells Centre not to introduce 3rd language in Class 9
content: "No that is very bad. 9th standard is stressful. Why do you introduce a new language in 9th? You introduce it in 6th. When we were there in our school we had both ICSE and SSLC. We were taught both syllabus. Only at the end of 9th standard we had to select," Justice BV Nagarathna, who was hearing plea challenging Madras high court direction to establish Jawahar Navodaya Vidyalayas in each district, said according to Live Law.

title: TNLEA 2026 rank list released at tnlea.com: Direct link to download here
content: The Directorate of Technical Education has released the Tamil Nadu Lateral Entry Admissions 2026 rank list. Eligible candidates can now check their ranks by logging into the official website. Special counselling for TNLEA 2026 has also begun for eligible candidates. This counselling process facilitates direct admission to engineering programs for diploma holders. Candidates must complete all required steps within the given schedule to secure their seats.

title: India's first hydrogen train set for launch: All you need to know
content: India will soon launch its first hydrogen fuel cell train on Friday. Prime Minister Narendra Modi will flag off the train in Haryana. This train generates its own electricity onboard using hydrogen fuel. The project establishes India's first integrated hydrogen railway ecosystem. This initiative marks a significant step towards cleaner transport solutions.

title: 'Every life is precious': Delhi HC urges Centre to regularly examine Wangchuk's health
content: Hearing the a PIL over fasting activist's dwindling health, the high court noted that ​​"life is precious" and asked both the governments to provide, if needed, medical aid, to the Ladakh native. Doctors have warned that Wangchuk may soon enter a potentially "alarming" phase involving organ damage. Wangchuk has reportedly lost more than 9kgs since beginning his indefinite hunger strike.

title: Lessons from Iran war? Saudi Arabia to buy 20,000 laser guided rockets to counter drones
content: The United States has greenlit a major arms transaction with Saudi Arabia, encompassing the sale of twenty thousand precision-guided rockets focused on air defense. This acquisition aims to bolster Saudi Arabia's operational capacity against ground and aerial threats, responding to security concerns raised by recent regional conflicts. Furthermore, the sale equips Saudi forces to effectively counter the increasing challenges posed by drone warfare.

title: Karnataka govt proposes new apartment bill: Why it matters for flat owners
content: Karnataka plans a new law to govern apartment ownership and management. This legislation will replace outdated acts from nineteen seventy-two. The proposed bill clarifies land and common area ownership for apartment owners. It also introduces mandatory structural checks for older buildings. A two-tier dispute resolution system will be established under the new law.

title: Scientists reveal why killer whales struggle to hear blue whales
content: Some baleen whales use low-frequency songs to avoid killer whales. This acoustic crypsis helps flight species escape predators in open oceans. Blue whales, known for loud calls, sing at frequencies predators cannot hear. Natural selection favored these low-frequency calls over millions of years. This discovery reveals how predation shaped whale communication systems.

title: Did Deepika Padukone delete an Instagram story backing Sonam Wangchuk amid hunger strike
content: A viral screenshot falsely claims Deepika Padukone supported Sonam Wangchuk's hunger strike. Verification shows no evidence of the actor posting or deleting such a story. The alleged Instagram post appears to be digitally manipulated and is unsubstantiated. Sonam Wangchuk continues his hunger strike at Jantar Mantar for examination reforms. Several film personalities have publicly expressed solidarity with the activist's ongoing protest.

title: US Fed enlists ex-RBI guv Rajan, 2 other Indians for massive policy overhaul
content: Former RBI governor Raghuram Rajan will help shape US monetary policy. He joins a Federal Reserve task force reviewing its policy framework. Rajan will examine balance sheet policy alongside other experts. Other panels will assess data, productivity, and inflation frameworks. These initiatives aim to improve the central bank's future policy approach.

title: Why Turkmenistan’s decades old ‘door to hell’ is fading
content: Turkmenistan's "door to hell" crater, a fiery landmark for decades, is significantly weakening. Authorities are actively reducing the natural gas flow feeding the persistent flames. This fiery spectacle, once a major tourist draw, is now an environmental concern. Scientists continue monitoring methane emissions from the site for climate research. The crater's future as a burning inferno appears uncertain.

title: Hyderabad school terminates teacher who allegedly gave 'Kalma' recitation as homework
content: A Hyderabad private school terminated a teacher after assigning religious homework. Parents of a Hindu student protested the school's instruction to recite Islamic prayers. The school issued a termination letter and permanently disqualified the teacher from future employment. BJP spokesperson Shehzad Poonawalla claimed his intervention led to the teacher's removal. The school has not publicly commented beyond issuing the termination letter.

title: Will gold head to Rs 1.45 lakh/10 gm & silver reach Rs 2.27 lakh per kg? Check outlook
content: Gold and silver price prediction today: Gold and silver prices are showing signs of recovery after a phase of weakness, says Abhilash Koikkara, Head - Forex & Commodities, Nuvama Professional Clients Group.

title: 17-Year-Old Leads 300 US Navy Cadets Before School, Nears Naval Officer Dream
content: Riddhi Chauhan, a 17-year-old Indian-American student from Queens, has secured admission to the Naval Academy Preparatory School, taking a step towards becoming a commissioned US Navy officer. As the Battalion Commanding Officer of her school’s NJROTC unit, she leads nearly 300 cadets through drills, training and mentoring activities before classes begin. Her journey includes multiple leadership roles, academic achievements and the development of her unit’s first SeaPerch underwater robot.

title: SC upholds Class I heirs' preferential right over inherited agricultural land
content: The Supreme Court has ruled that Section 22 of the Hindu Succession Act, 1956 applies to inherited agricultural land, reaffirming that Class I heirs enjoy a preferential right to purchase the share of another co-heir before it is transferred to a third party.

title: 'Mossad or CIA': Vance hints at Epstein-Israeli intel link; admits Trump admin 'screwed up'
content: JD Vance admitted that the Trump administration mishandled the communication surrounding the release of the Jeffrey Epstein files, blaming much of the confusion on former Attorney General Pam Bondi's public remarks. He also defended Donald Trump against allegations of trying to suppress the documents and suggested that intelligence agencies like Mossad or CIA had connections to Epstein.

title: As 100 ISRO Scientists Quit, Retire Early, Centre Tightens Exit Rule
content: An official directive acknowledged that there has been a "spate of requests" for voluntary retirement and resignation from Group 'A' Scientific/Technical personnel of ISRO.

title: 5 Years After Kerala Bank Scam, Rs 1 Crore 'Scam' Hits Cooperative Society
content: Sources familiar with the investigation told NDTV they suspect the alleged misappropriation of depositors' money could run into several crores, with only a handful of victims having approached the police so far.

title: Beyond Low Cost: Why Foreigners Choose India's Healthcare Over The West
content: India's medical tourism industry is estimated to be worth $12.32 billion in 2026. It is growing much faster than the country's overall tourism sector.

title: Centre Expands PM Internship Scheme: How Aspirants, Businesses Will Benefit
content: The Prime Minister Internship Scheme aims to provide internship opportunities to one crore young people over five years in India's leading companies.

title: Top Microsoft Executives Replaced In Major AI Overhaul: Report
content: Hayete Gallot who took over Microsoft's security business in February has replaced several senior executives as part of the company's AI restructuring, according to a report by The Information. Microsoft is the largest cybersecurity software seller in the world.

title: Mumbai Train Seat Row Turns Violent, Man Injured In Metal Bracelet Attack
content: The incident took place on an Ambernath-bound local train travelling between Dombivli and Thakurli stations shortly after midnight.

title: Probe Agency Raids 13 Locations In UP Over Rohingya Terror Funding Network
content: An organised gang was accused of arranging forged Indian identity documents and the illegal entry of Rohingya and Bangladeshi nationals into India.

title: IRCTC Launches New Ticket Booking Website: Check Key Features
content: The IRCTC website was first launched in 2002 and currently handles an average of 14.5 lakh train ticket bookings every day.

title: "Neglect Turned Into Natural Disaster": Father Of Boy Killed In Mumbai Tree Collapse
content: Vihaan Srivastav, the 11-year-old, was killed on the last day of June when a huge tree collapsed on his school bus in Mumbai's Chembur.

title: OnePlus Exits US, Europe As Oppo Reshapes Business: Is India Next In Line?
content: Oppo has been facing financial pressure across its smartphone business amid weak demand in overseas markets, including the US, Europe and even India.

title: Hyderabad Startup Plans 'Robotic Hug' To Clean Up Space Junk
content: The technology is aimed at solving one of the biggest challenges facing the space industry today: space junk.

title: PM Modi Pushes To Cut Import Reliance To Shield India From Shocks
content: Prime Minister's office ordered key ministries to identify categories of goods in which import dependence is high, and can be replaced by locally made products, according to officials familiar with the matter.

title: US, China Lead AI Race. Where India Stands And What's Really At Stake
content: The AI race isn't about prestige. Experts say AI is becoming critical infrastructure-just like electricity, telecom networks or cloud computing.

title: Delhi Cop Installing Speed Monitoring Device Gets Killed By Speeding Car
content: The incident took place at around 6 pm at the Singhu border in outer north Delhi when the policeman was installing a speed monitoring machine near a traffic booth.

title: Hyderabad Parents Claim School Told Student To Learn 'Kalma' For Homework
content: According to the family, their six-year-old child returned home with a homework diary containing instructions to read the 'Kalma' and 'Fateh'.

title: Russia Seeks More Fuel From India As Ukraine Attacks Refineries: Report
content: India is the biggest buyer of Russian seaborne crude oil, making Moscow's bid to secure Indian gasoline an unusual reversal in the countries' energy trade relationship.

title: 'Woman Can't Claim Maintenance From Step-Son After Relief From Real Son': Allahabad Court
content: Justice Laxmi Kant Shukla, in his judgement passed on Tuesday, said that after receiving maintenance from her real son, the mother is no longer incapable of maintaining herself and hence not entitled to any subsequent maintenance order in her favour.

title: 'People Will Take Oxygen Shots': Bombay High Court On Shrinking Green Cover
content: "It is a loss for this area. As it is, Bombay has so little oxygen that the day will not be far when people will carry oxygen cylinders to take oxygen shots," Bombay High Court remarked.

title: Indian Mother, 24, Stabbed To Death At UK Home, Family Calls It 'Hate Crime'
content: Kaur's family has alleged that the assailant entered her bedroom and attacked her with a sharp-edged weapon in what they believed was a racist attack.

title: Relief for bulk waste generators as Minister promises to end vendor monopoly in Bengaluru
content: Currently, BSWML has empanelled five companies, one for each corporation, to collect waste from bulk generators, which has led to a monopoly

title: Third language must stop in Class 9, not start in Class 9, Justice Nagarathna tells government
content: “Why introduce third language in Class 9? That is stressful. Why not Class 6?” Justice Nagarathna pointed out

title: Can’t English be considered an indigenous Indian language, asks Supreme Court
content: SC questions the use of the colonially loaded term ‘native’ in CBSE circular, says the push for three-language scheme may be in the spirit of the Constitutional goal to use Indian languages for official purposes

title: Kudankulam Nuclear Power Plant data leak: What happened and what we know | Explained
content: Files linked to Kudankulam Nuclear Power Plant surfaced on the Dark Web after a reported ransomware incident. This explainer breaks down what was leaked, what it means, and what remains protected

title: CBI arrests MD of Bengaluru-based Karnataka Antibiotics and Pharmaceuticals Limited for taking bribe of ₹5 lakh
content: Karnataka Antibiotics and Pharmaceuticals Limited (KAPL) Managing Director Anurag Danayak was allegedly caught red-handed taking the bribe from a company that is a service agent of KAPL, according to a CBI spokesperson

title: Delhi HC directs daily medical monitoring of Sonam Wangchuk amid indefinite hunger strike
content: The petition argued that the government could not remain a passive spectator, contending that failure to intervene could amount to ‘abetment of suicide’ under Section 108 of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023

title: Sonam Wangchuk loses over 9 kg, weakens significantly on 19th day of hunger strike: CJP
content: Sonam Wangchuk remains under constant medical supervision, the CJP said in a statement, adding that he is still ‘mentally alert’, even as he has weakened significantly

title: Bombay High Court seeks Centre’s response on comedian Kunal Kamra's plea against ‘Sahyog Portal’
content: Kunal Kamra had claimed that the Sahyog Portal was a tool used by the government for online censorship and the amended rules enabled the government to order the takedown of content without adequate safeguards

title: Six men arrested for stealing silver-laced soil from Hindustan Photo Films factory in Nilgiris
content: It is believed that sludge from producing the films would have trace amounts of gold and silver, which would then accrue in the soil in certain parts of the factory where they were stored. One of the arrested men is Suresh, the branch secretary of the TVK from Ward 2 in Udhagamandalam.

title: May rethink over delimitation Bill if Opposition's amendments incorporated: Sanjay Raut
content: Shiv Sena (UBT) MP Sanjay Raut, talking to reporters ahead of the Ram Raksha Andolan on July 18, says he will rethink delimation Bill if Oppostion’s demand for the Bill are met amid ongoing protests

title: Andhra Pradesh signs MoU with UN-Habitat for Inclusive urban development
content: The partnership aims to establish a Centre of Excellence for Urban Affairs in Amaravati and focus on alleviating urban poverty, improving livelihood of urban poor, and promoting inclusive city development

title: Free Trade Agreements, including the one with India and UK, offer favourable market access to MSMEs: FKCCI
content: For MSMEs, which number over 6.3 crore across India and contribute nearly 30% to the GDP, the effective use of FTAs can be truly transformational, said FKCCI President Uma Reddy.

title: Centre floats another draft of CAFE-III; gives special recognition to ethanol and bio fuel cars
content: Industry stakeholders have until August 6 to respond to the latest draft; the proposed CAFE norms have sharply divided the automobile industry, with manufacturers of smaller and larger vehicles taking opposing positions and competing proposals emerging over how the regulations should be framed

title: Mass fish kill: Two years on, Periyar fishers await compensation
content: Fishermen have urged the government to consider extending due compensation to the fisherfolk as their daily earnings were hit following the contamination of the river

title: CM V.D. Satheesan launches Thiruvonam Bumper Lottery with ₹30 crore first prize
content: Tickets to be sold from July 20, draw on September 26; the ticket price of the lottery remains unchanged at ₹500.

title: Monsoon season power crisis: Power curbs likely to continue in Kerala
content: Electricity Minister Sunny Joseph said that the State is currently facing a 500 MW to 600 MW deficit in availability

title: A new era of Indian ecology looks to its horizons, and to the ground
content: The second Indian Wildlife Ecology Conference at Ashoka University was a forum where researchers from universities, government agencies, NGOs, and field stations together figured out where the discipline is heading — using insights into evolutionary history, long-term monitoring, public policy, technology, and public health

title: Why are rival Trinamool Congress factions competing to hold the July 21 Martyrs’ Day Rally? | Explained
content: For more than three decades, the Martyrs’ Day rally in West Bengal has been synonymous with Mamata Banerjee. This year, rival Trinamool factions and the Congress are competing to claim its political legacy

title: Congress leaders discuss party strategy for upcoming Parliament session
content: Senior Congress leaders, including party chief Mallikarjun Kharge, Leader of Opposition in Lok Sabha Rahul Gandhi and other senior leaders deliberated on key issues to be taken up during the session.

title: An anthem for a vanishing craft: Thazhappattu makes it to school curriculum
content: Screwpine art, which involves transforming simple leaves into green, sustainable thazhappas (mats) — a craft that was once a thriving cottage industry in Thazhava but has slowly declined due to modern challenges – receives a significant tribute in the form of Thazhappattu, a song written by Ani Mank.

title: Raut urges Fadnavis to take part in 'Ram Raksha Andolan' of Sena (UBT) in Nagpur
content: Sena (UBT) head and former State CM Uddhav Thackeray had earlier said that he would take part in the Ram Raksha Andolan organised by the party in Nagpur, the hometown of CM Fadnavis, on Saturday (July 16).

title: Beyond momo: Meghalaya’s chefs are reclaiming the state’s culinary identity
content: In Shillong, chefs behind A’Origins, Rynsan and Lady Aiko are reclaiming Meghalaya’s culinary heritage with dishes built around fermented bamboo shoots, smoked meats, forest produce and traditional cooking techniques.

title: 12-year-old Bengaluru boy dies after collapsing in school; family gets CCTV camera footage one month later, alleges negligence
content: The CCTV camera footage reportedly shows the boy informing his teacher that he was feeling unwell before returning to the classroom. Moments later, he allegedly collapsed on his bench

title: Hurdles before oil exploration on Assam-Nagaland boundary | Explained
content: Extremist and traditional tribe-based organisations in Nagaland do not want oil majors to resume oil exploration and extraction until the inter-State border dispute and the ‘Indo-Naga political issue’ are resolved

title: Poor monsoon and no Cauvery water: distress in Tamil Nadu’s delta region
content: All is not well in the famed ‘Granary of South India’. Between a poor southwest  monsoon and lack of water in the Cauvery, crops and farmers are left high and dry. The kuruvai cultivation area in Thanjavur alone has dipped to around 89,000 acres this season from around 1,94,000 acres in the previous cultivation year.  This report describes the situation on the ground

title: Seating dispute sparks brawl on local train in Thane, three commuters injured
content: The incident occurred in the luggage compartment of the Ambernath-bound suburban train, Kalyan Government Railway Police's (GRP) says, senior inspector Archana Dusane

title: AI, automation to reshape maritime workforce as India eyes global shipping hub: Maritime University officials
content: Changing technologies, green shipping and automation are redefining career pathways in the maritime sector, experts say

title: Sabari Varman custodial death in Nagercoil jail: Chief warder, two warders arrested
content: Sabari Varman, 34, a differently abled resident of Eeththankaadu near Suchindram, was arrested on July 9 for allegedly possessing the banned gutka and remanded in custody.  He died in the prison  there under suspicious circumstances on July 12

title: Tirupati police get 66 electric bikes for patrolling; 13 for Shakti Teams
content: Collector S. Venkateswar and SP L. Subbarayudu flagged off the VIDA electric bikes gifted by Hero Motors; 53 will be deployed at the district’s 53 police stations and 13 with the women-safety Shakti Teams

title: India-U.K. DCC not applicable to Indians working in the U.K. before July 15
content: DCC came into effect on July 15, 2026 alongside the India-U.K. Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA)

title: 99% of exports to become duty-free with India-U.K. trade treaty: Amit Shah
content: Union Home Minister Amit Shah said the treaty stands as a testament to the Centre's pro-people diplomacy

title: Magisterial probe ordered into Ballia custodial death allegation; SP team to visit village
content: The Samajwadi Party said a 14-member delegation, constituted on the directions of party president Akhilesh Yadav, will visit Gaighat village on July 17 to express condolences to the bereaved family

title: India–UK CETA comes into force; Telangana well poised to reap benefits
content: Pharmaceuticals and Life Sciences, electronics and precision engineering, engineering goods and industrial machinery  and chemicals and specialty chemicals are set to benefit most from the landmark agreement that came into force on July 15

title: What are the gains from the India-U.K. trade deal? | Explained
content: Zero tariffs. Cheaper whisky and British cars. Major benefits for Indian workers in the U.K. The India-U.K. trade deal could reshape business on both sides. Here’s the complete picture

title: Nagercoil Sabari Varman’s custodial death: Eight prison inmates arrested
content: With the post mortem report revealing injuries on his body, the police have alerted the case of suspicious death to that of murder. However, the motive for the murder was still being probed

title: Provident Fund interest credited to all 34 crore accounts on July 15
content: The Union Finance Ministry had recently approved the recommendation of the EPFO’s Central Board of Trustees to provide interest at the rate of 8.25%; a senior EPFO official said the credited interest will be visible to all members when they open passbook for 2025-26.

title: Interpretation of sexual offences should not be limited only to physical act: NCW chief
content: “While interpreting sexual offences, the focus should not be limited only to the physical act. Equal importance must also be given to the survivor’s dignity, her consent, the fear she experienced, and the psychological trauma caused by the incident,” Ms. Rahatkar said.

title: Kanwariyas vandalise pickup after 'contact' with kanwar in Uttar Pradesh’s Muzaffarnagar
content: According to Station House Officer (SHO) Manvendra Singh Bhati, the pickup vehicle allegedly brushed against one of the 'kanwars', triggering anger among the pilgrims who allegedly vandalised the vehicle.

title: Protest in Shivamogga over allowing students of private university and engineering course to apply for Agriculture Officer post
content: KEA allowed students who had obtained a B.Sc. (Agriculture) degree from Rai Technology University, a private university, and those with a B.E. degree in biotechnology to apply for posts of Agriculture Officer and Assistant AO

title: Women's quota, Delimitation Bills will be passed in Monsoon Session: Athawale
content: We have a two-thirds majority. Women's Reservation Bill and Delimitation Bill will be passed, says Union Minister Ramdas Athawale

title: Sonam Wangchuk’s decision to join CJP protest organic, says wife Gitanjali Angmo
content: Sonam Wangchuk wanted to ensure that the CJP’s cause is not sabotaged by “nefarious” elements, and the movement does not go astray, says his wife Gitanjali J. Angmo

title: Trinamool MP Samirul Islam calls on family who was pushed into Bangladesh
content: Four Bengali migrants from Birbhum who were detained by Delhi Police last year and pushed into Bangladesh were brought back to India on July 8, 2026 after a year-long legal battle. It was Samirul Islam who took up the cause of the migrant workers and provided help to the family

title: What is the row over Karur ‘temple land’? | Explained
content: When Tamil Nadu CM Vijay visited Karur on July 10, activist T.R. Ramesh accused the TVK government and the HR&CE Department of handing over the lands in question —valued at around ₹25,000 crore— to “encroachers"

title: Nagpur Cyber Police register FIR against content creators over alleged defamatory campaign targeting Nitin Gadkari
content: The legal action stems from highly contested online posts regarding the government's Ethanol-blended (E20) fuel policy

title: Kannada social media influencer booked for sexual harassment in Bengaluru
content: According to the complaint, the alleged incident took place during the promotion of a pub, where Manju had reportedly consumed alcohol and behaved inappropriately with a female server

title: Supreme Court to hear appeal against ban on animated film Mahaprabhu Jagannath for not being true to ‘Skanda Purana’
content: In an oral mentioning, senior advocate Devadatt Kamat, representing the film’s makers, Ele Animations, said the movie was due for release on July 17 in 300-odd theatres across the country

title: ED searches 13 locations in alleged terror-funding and illegal infiltration case
content: The case is linked to a First Information Report (FIR) registered by the Anti-Terror Squad (ATS) of Uttar Pradesh. The searches were carried out in Delhi, Uttar Pradesh, Haryana, West Bengal, and Maharashtra

title: Farmers stage motorcycle rally to protest against proposed India-U.S. trade deal
content: Farmers behind the ‘Desh Bachao Morcha’ urge the Central government to ‘prioritise the interests of farmers, workers, small traders, small industries and India’s economic and food sovereignty, and refrain from implementing any agreement that goes against the interests of the people’

title: No shortage of fertilisers in NTR district, says Collector
content: Lakshmisha says the government follows a transparent fertiliser distribution system through the A.P. Agribusiness Information Management System app, and fertilisers are given to farmers based on online land records

title: US weighs $100,000 bond for some Green Card applicants abroad: What it means for Indians
content: the proposal is still under discussion within the US State Department . it is part of a push to tighten legal immigration and ensure new immigrants are financially self-sufficient . if implemented, the policy would apply to certain applicants seeking immigrant visas at US consulates abroad .
